old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-v90","title":"Equipment parser: handle intelligent item stat blocks (41 items)","description":"41 equipment items have intelligent item stat blocks separated by \u003chr\u003e tags that the parser doesn't handle. Pattern: after Usage/Bulk line, \u003chr\u003e introduces Perception, Communication, Skills, Int/Wis/Cha, Will fields, then another \u003chr\u003e before description. These are creature-like stat blocks that need new fields extracted into the JSON. IDs: 595, 596, 597, 618, 666, 1177-1180, 1590, 1725, 1743, 1748, 2376, 2382, 2392-2404, 2612, 3123, 3125-3127, 3750-3755, 3843, 4001","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:03:44Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:03:44Z"}

{"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-c2i","title":"Special sense parsing errors: duplicated/merged names, languages as senses","description":"Multiple special_sense name parsing failures in monsters/ and npcs/:
- "darkvisiondarkvision" (4 files): kellid_graveknight.json, rezatha.json (kingmaker_adventure_path) + 2 others

- "Light Blindness" vs "light blindness" casing: xotanispawn.json
- Empty sense "": ekundayo_level_6.json (NPC)
- "plus vigilance" as sense: elven_court_guard.json (NPC)
Root cause: Kingmaker AP HTML likely has unusual formatting causing language/sense merge. Other files have range/value parsing edge cases.

{"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-3rs","title":"Structured content leaked into section text: afflictions, spells, attacks","description":"Full game stat blocks found in section.text that should be structured data:

AFFLICTIONS in section text (2 files):
- arctic_bloom.json (gatewalkers_hardcover) — "Paralyzing Poison" with DC 19, stages
- yaiafineti.json (pathfinder_189) — same affliction

SPELLS in section text (4 files, 5 occurrences):
- arcane_living_rune.json (bestiary_3) — "Divine Innate Spells" DC 35
- kasesh.json (impossible_lands) — "Primal Innate Spells"
- mosquito_witch.json (monsters_of_myth) — "Occult Innate Spells" + "Primal Innate Spells" in 2 sections

ATTACKS in section text (1 file):
- tehialai-thief-of-ships.json (monsters_of_myth) — "Melee barbed spear +27"

These represent alternate creature stat blocks described in lore sections that the parser does not currently extract. May require special handling for multi-variant creatures.

Also: greater_barghest.json (bestiary) Mutations offensive ability has frequency/trigger/effect in text but not as structured fields.

new_value:
{"notes":"Analysis: All 7 files are parser issues, not HTML bugs. The parser only recognizes activation traits when inside parentheses. These items have traits outside parens:
- 'or more' (E 461, E 1046): action flexibility text before parens
- fortune/mental (E 2139 x2): trait links as comma-sep list before semicolon
- healing/vitality (E 2842): trait links after action icon, no parens
- focus (E 588): plain trait word after action icon, no parens
Fix requires _extract_activation_details() to recognize trait links (and known trait words like 'focus') outside parentheses as traits, not activation_types.","status":"open"}

These need either proper bulk values or a modifier/note field.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T00:51:02Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-03-06T00:51:02Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Analysis: These are legitimate game content. Items like Diplomat's Badge activate via 'Recall Knowledge', Fear Gem via 'Intimidating Strike', etc. The HTML correctly shows the feat/action name as the activation. Design decision: should these be in activation_type alongside standard types (interact, envision, command), or separated into a different field? Currently the parser faithfully represents the HTML."}
old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-zr3","title":"Activation type has feat/action names instead of standard types (~30 files)","description":"activation_type field contains specific feat or action names (final surge, recall knowledge, intimidating strike, knockdown, brutal finish, certain strike, climb, investigate, raise a shield, strengthen resolve, administer first aid, treat poison, treat wounds, seek, interact or strike, land with grace, reflect curse, shift scales, speak with undead, make an impression, close at hand, interact or 10 minutes, interact auditory, cast a spell or perform ritual). These are the specific actions required to activate — may need a different field or structured representation vs the standard activation_type enum (interact, envision, command, etc.).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T00:50:32Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-03-06T00:50:32Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Analysis: 'see below' (impact_foam_chassis) and 'varies' (winders_ring) are what the HTML says. The tattoo items (unifying_emblem x2) have descriptive text about activation. These are legitimate edge cases in the HTML. Design decision: parser could strip these or add a flag."}

new_value: Leveraged variant system for Briar's progressive intelligent item. HTML fix: converted hr/bold sharpness point sections to h2 headers. Parser: expanded variant detection to match Sharpness Point h2s, added _extract_inline_intelligent_item() for variant-level intelligent item extraction. All 4 tiers (4/8/14/20 SP) now have structured intelligent_item objects.

Fixed _normalize_bulk to split compound bulk values: '- varies' → text='varies', '- when not activated' → text='-' with modifier, '- L (...)' → text='L' with modifier. 3 remaining items (legacy magic_armor 'varies by armor', heckling_tools 'as tools of the normal type') are genuinely descriptive text that can't be further structured.
All 9 cardinality audit tickets closed. Equipment parser now at 0 errors across all 6 types (equipment, armor, shield, weapon, siege_weapon, vehicle).

Fixed _extract_action_type_from_spans to detect connector text ('to' vs 'or') between action spans. All 5 items with [one-action] to [three-actions] now correctly produce 'One to Three Actions'. Also added 'or more' handling for [one-action] or more pattern (2 items).

{"description":"Add a boolean parameter (e.g., allow_multiple=False) to handle_alternate_link() in universal/universal.py so it can return either a single object or an array of alternate links.

Currently spells use a custom _extract_alternate_links() because handle_alternate_link() asserts len(links) == 1. Nine spells have multiple alternate links (spell split/merge during remaster, e.g., Cleanse Affliction -> Neutralize Poison + Remove Disease + Remove Curse).

When allow_multiple=True:
- Return single object for 1 link (same as current behavior)
- Return array for multiple links

This would let spell.py use the universal function instead of a custom reimplementation. Related to PFSRD2-Parser-qk7 (universal alternate_link pass)."}

old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-j7m","title":"Migrate creature/equipment/monster_ability trait_db_pass to universal","description":"trait_db_pass now lives in pfsrd2/sql/traits.py with a pre_process hook for parser-specific logic. Three parsers still have their own copies: creatures.py (alignment splitting, value extraction), equipment.py (alignment abbreviations, name fixes, value splitting), monster_ability.py (simple copy). Migrate each to use the shared function, passing parser-specific logic via the pre_process callback.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:43:07Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:43:07Z"}

new_value: Migrated creatures.py, equipment.py, monster_ability.py to use universal trait_db_pass with pre_process hooks. Updated pre_process signature to (trait, parent, curs). Added classes sorting and edition-optional handling to universal version.
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-fg7","title":"Add schema copy to pf2_run_*.sh scripts","description":"Each pf2_run_*.sh should copy its schema file to pfsrd2-data after a successful run. Schema filenames include the version (e.g., spell.schema.2.0.json), so the script needs to extract the version from the schema (jq '.schema_version' or similar) and construct the output filename accordingly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:52:54Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:52:54Z"}

new_value: Created copy_schema.sh helper script. Added schema copy to all 10 run scripts (spells, skills, conditions, creatures, npcs, traits, monster_abilities, sources, armor_groups, equipment, weapon_groups). Weapon groups use armor_group schema.
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-g6i","title":"Align monster_ability schema trait definition with other schemas","description":"Pre-existing inconsistency: monster_ability.schema.json trait definition is missing 'sections' and does not require 'edition', unlike creature, equipment, skill, and spell schemas. Discovered during PR #75 review by schema-consistency-reviewer agent.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:29Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:29Z"}

new_value: Added sections to properties, added edition to required, removed text from required — now matches all other schemas
All parsers fixed for HTML5 migration
Creatures parser fixed for HTML5, 0 errors
Complete. 8142/8142 feats parsed, schema v1.0 validated, 42 unit tests.

old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-cij","title":"Extract Special field from post-hr feat description","description":"538 feats have **Special** in description text but it's never extracted as a structured field. Special always appears after the \u003chr\u003e separator, so _extract_bold_fields (which only processes pre-hr text) never sees it. Need to extract Special from post-hr text as well.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"devon.jones@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-08T17:21:11Z","created_by":"Devon Jones","updated_at":"2026-03-08T17:21:11Z"}

old_value: {"id":"PFSRD2-Parser-eux","title":"Extract post-hr Trigger/Requirement from feat descriptions","description":"39 feats have **Trigger** and 2 have **Requirement** appearing in post-hr description text, not extracted into structured fields.
